prompt:
You are the SCAFFOLDER (opus) in an autonomous substrate-build loop. You build a generic, reusable Lean substrate module.

## Requirement
# Substrate requirement: gaussian_kl

## Goal
Compute the closed-form Kullback‚ÄďLeibler divergence between two real Gaussian
measures (`ProbabilityTheory.gaussianReal`), which Mathlib does not provide.

## Provides (API contract)
Using Mathlib's `gaussianReal m v` (mean `m : ‚ĄĚ`, variance `v : ‚ĄĚ‚Č•0`) and
`MeasureTheory.klDiv` (value in `‚ĄĚ‚Č•0‚ąě`):
- `gaussianKL_eq` (REQUIRED ‚ÄĒ the reusable workhorse): for equal variance `v` with
  `0 < v`,
      `klDiv (gaussianReal m‚āÄ v) (gaussianReal m‚āĀ v)
         = ENNReal.ofReal ((m‚āÄ - m‚āĀ)^2 / (2 * (v : ‚ĄĚ)))`.
  This is the canonical KL input for Gaussian-location minimax lower bounds.
- `gaussianKL_general` (OPTIONAL stretch ‚ÄĒ only if it falls out cleanly): for
  `0 < v‚āÄ`, `0 < v‚āĀ`,
      `klDiv (gaussianReal m‚āÄ v‚āÄ) (gaussianReal m‚āĀ v‚āĀ)
         = ENNReal.ofReal ((1/2) * (Real.log (v‚āĀ/v‚āÄ) + v‚āÄ/v‚āĀ
             + (m‚āÄ - m‚āĀ)^2 / v‚āĀ - 1))`.
  Skip / escalate this one if it needs substantially more than the equal-variance
  proof; `gaussianKL_eq` alone fulfills the goal.

## Statement / milestones (equal-variance route ‚ÄĒ a sketch, not a mandate)
1. Absolute continuity: `gaussianReal m‚āÄ v ‚Č™ gaussianReal m‚āĀ v` (both are
   `volume.withDensity (gaussianPDF ¬∑ v)` with a strictly positive pdf, hence
   mutually absolutely continuous with `volume`).
2. Log-likelihood ratio: `llr (gaussianReal m‚āÄ v) (gaussianReal m‚āĀ v)` is a.e. equal
   to `x ‚Ü¶ (m‚āÄ - m‚āĀ) * (2*x - m‚āÄ - m‚āĀ) / (2*v)` ‚ÄĒ the difference of the two Gaussian
   log-densities (the normalizing constants cancel at equal variance; `rnDeriv` ratio
   = `gaussianPDF m‚āÄ v / gaussianPDF m‚āĀ v`).
3. Integrability of that llr under `gaussianReal m‚āÄ v` (it is affine in `x`, and the
   Gaussian has finite first moment).
4. `klDiv = ‚ąę llr ‚ąā(gaussianReal m‚āÄ v)`; evaluate using `‚ąę x ‚ąāgaussianReal m‚āÄ v = m‚āÄ`
   (`integral_id_gaussianReal`):  `(m‚āÄ-m‚āĀ)¬∑(2 m‚āÄ - m‚āÄ - m‚āĀ)/(2 v) = (m‚āÄ-m‚āĀ)^2/(2 v)`.
5. Package as `ENNReal.ofReal` of the (nonnegative) closed form.

(The proof route is the scaffolder's call ‚ÄĒ any sound, sorry-free derivation of the
stated equality is acceptable. The general formula additionally needs the second
moment / variance of the Gaussian and the `log (v‚āĀ/v‚āÄ)` term.)

## Standard reference
Standard closed form (any information-theory / statistics text), e.g. Cover & Thomas
or the Gaussian-KL identity `KL(N(m‚āÄ,ŌÉ¬≤) ‚ą• N(m‚āĀ,ŌÉ¬≤)) = (m‚āÄ‚ąím‚āĀ)¬≤/(2ŌÉ¬≤)`; general two-
variance form `KL = ¬Ĺ(log(ŌÉ‚āĀ¬≤/ŌÉ‚āÄ¬≤) + ŌÉ‚āÄ¬≤/ŌÉ‚āĀ¬≤ + (m‚āÄ‚ąím‚āĀ)¬≤/ŌÉ‚āĀ¬≤ ‚ąí 1)`. FETCH a reference to
confirm the exact general-form constants if attempting the stretch goal.

## Intended reuse
The canonical KL input for Gaussian-location minimax lower bounds: feed `gaussianKL_eq`
into the existing `Causalean.Stat.klForm_two_point_lower_bound` (and the iid product-KL
tensorization) to obtain Le Cam / two-point converses for any Gaussian-noise estimation
problem (Gaussian sequence model, normal-means, Gaussian-noise dose-response). Must be
generic in `m‚āÄ, m‚āĀ, v`.

## May assume / must derive
- MAY assume: `0 < v` (nondegenerate Gaussian; for the general case `0 < v‚āÄ, 0 < v‚āĀ`).
- MUST derive (no `sorry`, no assumed gaussian-KL gate): the absolute continuity, the
  llr formula, the integrability, and the integral evaluation ‚ÄĒ from Mathlib's
  `gaussianReal` / `klDiv` API. Do NOT assume the closed form (or `klDiv`'s value) as a
  black-box hypothesis.

## Non-goals
- No multivariate / `EuclideanSpace` Gaussian KL (real `gaussianReal` only).
- No degenerate `v = 0` (Dirac) case.
- Do NOT re-prove Mathlib's Gaussian API (`rnDeriv_gaussianReal`, `integral_id_gaussianReal`,
  `gaussianPDF` facts) ‚ÄĒ import and use them.

## Known building blocks
- `ProbabilityTheory.gaussianReal`, `gaussianReal_of_var_ne_zero`
  (`= volume.withDensity (gaussianPDF őľ v)`), `gaussianPDF`, `gaussianPDFReal`,
  `rnDeriv_gaussianReal` (`‚ąā(gaussianReal őľ v)/‚ąāvolume =ŠĶÉňĘ gaussianPDF őľ v`),
  `integral_id_gaussianReal` (`‚ąę x ‚ąāgaussianReal őľ v = őľ`), `noAtoms_gaussianReal`.
- `MeasureTheory.klDiv`, `klDiv_eq_integral_klFun` / `klDiv_of_ac_of_integrable`,
  `ProbabilityTheory.llr`, `llr_def`, and `Measure.rnDeriv` / `withDensity` lemmas for
  absolute continuity.
- `Real.log_div`, `Real.log_mul`, `Real.log_exp`, basic `integral` linearity.

## Target module (optional)
Causalean.Mathlib.InformationTheory.GaussianKL


## Workspace
- Write/extend Lean files under: /<repo-root>/CausalSmith/CausalSmith/Substrate/GaussianKl
- Module prefix (one file per module): CausalSmith.Substrate.GaussianKl.<Name>
- This is build round 1 of at most 10.

## Your task this turn
0. ASSESS CURRENT STATUS FROM GROUND TRUTH. You are a fresh invocation with NO memory of prior turns ‚ÄĒ your only context is this prompt plus what you read from disk. Read the current files under the Lean dir and check their real state (remaining `sorry`s and any errors) via lean-lsp diagnostics or a targeted build. Trust the files over the prior round report (a report can lag a partial filler edit). The report and your prior plan below tell you the INTENT; the files tell you the TRUTH.
1. Search the existing library FIRST (run `npm run search -- "<concept>"` in CausalSmith/tools; use lean-lsp local/leansearch) to reuse, not reinvent.
1b. FETCH THE PRIMARY SOURCE when possible. If the Requirement names a reference (or you find the canonical one), fetch it to ground the exact statement, hypotheses, and naming/conventions you formalize ‚ÄĒ via WebFetch/WebSearch or Bash `curl`/`wget`; for an arXiv paper download the LaTeX source and read the `.tex`, not the PDF. Skip only when no source is named or none is reachable; never block on it.
2. UPDATE `plan_markdown` as a STRUCTURED STATUS LEDGER (this IS the memory the next turn inherits ‚ÄĒ make it concrete, not a vision doc). Use these sections: `## Done` (decls proven/closed), `## Remaining` (the open sorries, by file), `## Blocked` (what's stuck and why), `## Decisions` (design/API choices + what was tried and rejected, so the next turn doesn't repeat dead ends).
3. WRITE or EXTEND the Lean scaffold: precise statements with `sorry` for unproven proofs. The file(s) MUST compile with only `sorry` warnings (no hard errors). Keep each file focused (<300 lines) with a `/-! -/` module docstring and a docstring on each public declaration (first paragraph = self-contained NL translation).
4. DECIDE how many codex subagents to use to fill the sorries this round, and emit ONE prompt per subagent (partition the sorries as you judge best).

## Integrity (HARD RULES ‚ÄĒ non-negotiable)
- Do NOT LAUNDER. Never weaken, narrow, or special-case a statement, and never delete or relax a hypothesis that should be proven, just to make the build pass or satisfy the reviewer. The statements must stay the genuine, general results the Requirement asks for. Strengthening an assumption to dodge a hard case, replacing the real goal with a corollary, or hiding the difficulty in a definition all count as laundering ‚ÄĒ do not do it.
- Do NOT create VACUOUS statements. No trivially-true claims, no `True`-like lemmas, no uninhabitable/contradictory hypotheses, no premises that are never satisfiable (an always-false hypothesis makes any conclusion hold for free). Every theorem must have real content and be applicable to the intended inhabited inputs.
- When revising after a failed review, FIX THE PROOF / API ‚ÄĒ not the goalposts. Address the findings without diluting what the module proves.
- If a clean, true, non-vacuous statement is genuinely out of reach, choose decision="escalate" with the reason. Escalating is correct; laundering or shipping a vacuous statement is a failure.

## Decisions
- decision="build": you wrote/updated the scaffold and want the fillers to run this round. Provide `codex_prompts` (>=1).
- decision="review": choose this ONLY after you have VERIFIED from ground truth that the module compiles with ZERO remaining sorries and zero errors (confirm via diagnostics/build this turn ‚ÄĒ do not infer it from the report) AND the statements are the genuine, non-vacuous results (no laundering). `codex_prompts` may be empty.
- decision="escalate": the goal is wrong, unrealistic, or only reachable by weakening it into something vacuous; set `escalation.reason`. The run halts.

Emit ONLY the StructuredOutput JSON object: { decision, plan_markdown, codex_prompts: [{id, target_decls, prompt}], escalation? }.
`plan_markdown` is your living status ledger (persisted to plan.md and fed back to you next turn) ‚ÄĒ keep it accurate.

  - `MeasureTheory.llr_def : llr őľ őĹ = fun x ‚Ü¶ log (őľ.rnDeriv őĹ x).toReal`.
  - rnDeriv chain: `Measure.rnDeriv_mul_rnDeriv` (`őľ.rnDeriv őĹ * őĹ.rnDeriv őļ =ŠĶź[őļ] őľ.rnDeriv őļ`, needs `őľ ‚Č™ őĹ`), `Measure.rnDeriv_withDensity_right` (`őľ.rnDeriv (őĹ.withDensity f) =ŠĶź[őĹ] (f ¬∑)‚ĀĽ¬Ļ * őľ.rnDeriv őĹ`, needs f a.e. ‚Č†0, ‚Č†‚ąě).
  - `probReal_univ : őľ.real Set.univ = 1` for probability measures (NOTE: `measureReal_univ_eq_one` is the DEPRECATED alias ‚ÄĒ use `probReal_univ`).

## Remaining (open sorries in Basic.lean, by line)
- L69 `rnDeriv_toReal_gaussianReal_ae`: `((gaussianReal m‚āÄ v).rnDeriv (gaussianReal m‚āĀ v) ¬∑).toReal =ŠĶź[gaussianReal m‚āÄ v] gaussianPDFReal m‚āÄ v ¬∑ / gaussianPDFReal m‚āĀ v ¬∑`.
- L81 `llr_gaussianReal_ae`: llr `=ŠĶź[gaussianReal m‚āÄ v] x ‚Ü¶ (m‚āÄ-m‚āĀ)*(2x-m‚āÄ-m‚āĀ)/(2v)`.
- L90 `integrable_llr_gaussianReal`: `Integrable (llr ...) (gaussianReal m‚āÄ v)`.
- L97 `integral_llr_gaussianReal`: `‚ąę llr ‚ąā(gaussianReal m‚āÄ v) = (m‚āÄ-m‚āĀ)¬≤/(2v)`.
- L109 `gaussianKL_eq` (MAIN): `klDiv ... = ENNReal.ofReal ((m‚āÄ-m‚āĀ)¬≤/(2v))`, `0 < v`.

## Blocked
- None.

## Decisions
- Use `v ‚Č† 0` in helper lemmas; main `gaussianKL_eq` takes `0 < v` and derives `hv : v ‚Č† 0` via `hv.ne'`.
- rnDeriv-ratio route (chosen, not yet verified end-to-end): write both Gaussians as `volume.withDensity (gaussianPDF ¬∑ v)`; compute `(gaussianReal m‚āÄ v).rnDeriv (gaussianReal m‚āĀ v)` a.e. via `rnDeriv_mul_rnDeriv` (őļ = gaussianReal m‚āĀ v, őľ = gaussianReal m‚āÄ v, őĹ = volume, needs `gaussianReal m‚āÄ v ‚Č™ volume`): `őľ.rnDeriv volume * volume.rnDeriv őĹ =ŠĶź[őĹ] őľ.rnDeriv őĹ`. All a.e. statements transfer to `=ŠĶź[gaussianReal m‚āÄ v]` since `gaussianReal m‚āÄ v ‚Č™ volume` AND `‚Č™ gaussianReal m‚āĀ v`. Then `.toReal` of `ofReal a / ofReal b` with a,b>0 gives `gaussianPDFReal m‚āÄ v / gaussianPDFReal m‚āĀ v`. ALTERNATIVE if chain is painful: `Measure.rnDeriv` via `withDensity_rnDeriv` lemmas, or prove the ratio directly from `gaussianReal_apply_eq_integral`/`setLIntegral`. - Skipping the OPTIONAL `gaussianKL_general` (two-variance form) ‚ÄĒ Requirement marks it as a stretch and says `gaussianKL_eq` alone fulfils the goal; not adding a sorry for it to keep the module focused.

Context: `open MeasureTheory ProbabilityTheory Real`, `open scoped NNReal ENNReal`. `gaussianReal m v : Measure ‚ĄĚ` is Mathlib's real Gaussian; for `v ‚Č† 0` it equals `volume.withDensity (gaussianPDF m v)` and is a probability measure. `klDiv` is `InformationTheory.klDiv`, `llr` is `MeasureTheory.llr`.

GOAL of the module: prove `gaussianKL_eq : klDiv (gaussianReal m‚āÄ v) (gaussianReal m‚āĀ v) = ENNReal.ofReal ((m‚āÄ-m‚āĀ)^2/(2*v))` for `0 < v`.

Proof route (a sketch ‚ÄĒ any sound route that keeps the statements is fine):

(1) `rnDeriv_toReal_gaussianReal_ae` (L69): show `((gaussianReal m‚āÄ v).rnDeriv (gaussianReal m‚āĀ v) x).toReal =ŠĶź[gaussianReal m‚āÄ v] gaussianPDFReal m‚āÄ v x / gaussianPDFReal m‚āĀ v x`. Compute the ‚ĄĚ‚Č•0‚ąě rnDeriv first. Key Mathlib lemmas: `Measure.rnDeriv_mul_rnDeriv (hőľőĹ : őľ ‚Č™ őĹ) : őľ.rnDeriv őĹ * őĹ.rnDeriv őļ =ŠĶź[őļ] őľ.rnDeriv őļ`; `ProbabilityTheory.rnDeriv_gaussianReal m v : ‚ąā(gaussianReal m v)/‚ąāvolume =ŠĶź[volume] gaussianPDF m v`; `Measure.rnDeriv_withDensity_right (őľ őĹ) (hf) (hf_ne_zero) (hf_ne_top) : őľ.rnDeriv (őĹ.withDensity f) =ŠĶź[őĹ] fun x ‚Ü¶ (f x)‚ĀĽ¬Ļ * őľ.rnDeriv őĹ x` (apply with őľ=őĹ=volume, f=gaussianPDF m‚āĀ v; `volume.rnDeriv volume =ŠĶź 1` via `Measure.rnDeriv_self`); `gaussianReal_of_var_ne_zero`. Use `gaussianReal_absolutelyContinuous m‚āÄ hv : gaussianReal m‚āÄ v ‚Č™ volume`. All these volume/őĹ a.e. equalities transfer to `=ŠĶź[gaussianReal m‚āÄ v]` because `gaussianReal m‚āÄ v ‚Č™ volume` (`Measure.AbsolutelyContinuous.ae_eq` / `Filter.EventuallyEq.filter_mono` with `ae_mono`). For `.toReal`: `gaussianPDF m v x = ENNReal.ofReal (gaussianPDFReal m v x)` by def (`gaussianPDF`), `gaussianPDFReal_pos m‚āĀ v x hv` gives positivity so `ENNReal.toReal_div`/`ENNReal.toReal_ofReal` apply (both pdfs > 0, finite). It may be cleaner to combine steps (2)+(1): if computing `.toReal` is fiddly, you can instead prove `llr_gaussianReal_ae` directly from the ‚ĄĚ‚Č•0‚ąě rnDeriv. Use `lean_multi_attempt`/`lean_state_search` to find the exact `toReal_div`/`toReal_inv`/`toReal_mul` names.

(2) `llr_gaussianReal_ae` (L81): `llr őľ őĹ x = log ((őľ.rnDeriv őĹ x).toReal)` (`llr_def`). Using (1), `llr =ŠĶź x ‚Ü¶ log (gaussianPDFReal m‚āÄ v x / gaussianPDFReal m‚āĀ v x)`. Now `gaussianPDFReal m v x = (‚ąö(2*ŌÄ*v))‚ĀĽ¬Ļ * rexp(-(x-m)^2/(2*v))`. The `(‚ąö(2ŌÄv))‚ĀĽ¬Ļ` factors are identical (equal variance) and cancel in the quotient; `Real.log_div` (args ‚Č† 0, use `(gaussianPDFReal_pos ..).ne'`), `Real.log_mul`, `Real.log_exp`. After simplification the log equals `(-(x-m‚āÄ)^2 + (x-m‚āĀ)^2)/(2*v)`. Conclude `= (m‚āÄ-m‚āĀ)*(2*x-m‚āÄ-m‚āĀ)/(2*v)` by `ring` (note `(x-m‚āĀ)^2 - (x-m‚āÄ)^2 = (m‚āÄ-m‚āĀ)*(2*x-m‚āÄ-m‚āĀ)`). Note `(v:‚ĄĚ) > 0` so `2*v ‚Č† 0`.

(3) `integrable_llr_gaussianReal` (L90): use `(integrable_congr (llr_gaussianReal_ae m‚āÄ m‚āĀ hv)).mpr ?_` to reduce to integrability of the affine `x ‚Ü¶ (m‚āÄ-m‚āĀ)*(2*x-m‚āÄ-m‚āĀ)/(2*v)` under `gaussianReal m‚āÄ v`. That is a constant times `(2*x - (m‚āÄ+m‚āĀ))`; `Integrable id (gaussianReal m‚āÄ v)` comes from `(memLp_id_gaussianReal (őľ:=m‚āÄ) (v:=v) 1).integrable (by norm_num)` (or `le_rfl`); combine with `Integrable.const_mul`, `.add`/`.sub`, `integrable_const`. `id = fun x ‚Ü¶ x`. Massage with `simp only [...]`/`ring_nf` or rewrite the function into `fun x ‚Ü¶ c * x + d` shape first.

(4) `integral_llr_gaussianReal` (L97): `rw [integral_congr_ae (llr_gaussianReal_ae m‚āÄ m‚āĀ hv)]`, then integrate the affine function. Split the constant `1/(2*v)*(m‚āÄ-m‚āĀ)` out (`integral_const_mul`/`integral_mul_left`/`MeasureTheory.integral_div`), `integral_sub`/`integral_add` (needs integrability of pieces ‚ÄĒ `Integrable id` as in (3), `integrable_const`), `integral_id_gaussianReal : ‚ąę x ‚ąāgaussianReal m‚āÄ v = m‚āÄ`, and `‚ąę (c:‚ĄĚ) ‚ąāgaussianReal m‚āÄ v = c` since it's a probability measure (`integral_const`, `measure_univ`, `ENNReal.toReal_one`, `smul_eq_mul`, `mul_one`). Result `(m‚āÄ-m‚āĀ)*(2*m‚āÄ-m‚āÄ-m‚āĀ)/(2*v) = (m‚āÄ-m‚āĀ)^2/(2*v)` by `ring`/`field_simp`.

(5) `gaussianKL_eq` (L109): set `hv0 : v ‚Č† 0 := hv.ne'`. `rw [InformationTheory.klDiv_of_ac_of_integrable (gaussianReal_ac_gaussianReal m‚āÄ m‚āĀ hv0) (integrable_llr_gaussianReal m‚āÄ m‚āĀ hv0)]`. Then the argument of `ENNReal.ofReal` is `‚ąę llr ‚ąā(gaussianReal m‚āÄ v) + (gaussianReal m‚āĀ v).real Set.univ - (gaussianReal m‚āÄ v).real Set.univ`. Both `.real Set.univ = 1` via `probReal_univ` (these ARE probability measures; `measureReal_univ_eq_one` is the deprecated alias ‚ÄĒ prefer `probReal_univ`). So it becomes `‚ąę llr + 1 - 1 = ‚ąę llr`. `rw [integral_llr_gaussianReal m‚āÄ m‚āĀ hv0]` (or `congr 1; ...`) to reach `ENNReal.ofReal ((m‚āÄ-m‚āĀ)^2/(2*v))`. Use `simp`/`ring_nf` to discharge `+1-1`.

Use `lean_hover_info` to confirm exact signatures (argument order of `rnDeriv_withDensity_right`, `klDiv_of_ac_of_integrable`, `probReal_univ`), `lean_local_search`/`lean_loogle` to find any lemma whose exact name you are unsure of, and `lean_multi_attempt` to test tactic blocks before committing. Do NOT add new axioms, do NOT weaken statements, do NOT introduce `sorry` anywhere. Final success criterion: `lean_diagnostic_messages` on the file returns no errors and no `sorry` warnings."}]}

GOAL of the module: prove `gaussianKL_eq : klDiv (gaussianReal m‚āÄ v) (gaussianReal m‚āĀ v) = ENNReal.ofReal ((m‚āÄ-m‚āĀ)^2/(2*v))` for `0 < v`.

Proof route (a sketch ‚ÄĒ any sound route that keeps the statements is fine):

(1) `rnDeriv_toReal_gaussianReal_ae` (L69): show `((gaussianReal m‚āÄ v).rnDeriv (gaussianReal m‚āĀ v) x).toReal =ŠĶź[gaussianReal m‚āÄ v] gaussianPDFReal m‚āÄ v x / gaussianPDFReal m‚āĀ v x`. Compute the ‚ĄĚ‚Č•0‚ąě rnDeriv first. Key Mathlib lemmas: `Measure.rnDeriv_mul_rnDeriv (hőľőĹ : őľ ‚Č™ őĹ) : őľ.rnDeriv őĹ * őĹ.rnDeriv őļ =ŠĶź[őļ] őľ.rnDeriv őļ`; `ProbabilityTheory.rnDeriv_gaussianReal m v : ‚ąā(gaussianReal m v)/‚ąāvolume =ŠĶź[volume] gaussianPDF m v`; `Measure.rnDeriv_withDensity_right (őľ őĹ) (hf) (hf_ne_zero) (hf_ne_top) : őľ.rnDeriv (őĹ.withDensity f) =ŠĶź[őĹ] fun x ‚Ü¶ (f x)‚ĀĽ¬Ļ * őľ.rnDeriv őĹ x` (apply with őľ=őĹ=volume, f=gaussianPDF m‚āĀ v; `volume.rnDeriv volume =ŠĶź 1` via `Measure.rnDeriv_self`); `gaussianReal_of_var_ne_zero`. Use `gaussianReal_absolutelyContinuous m‚āÄ hv : gaussianReal m‚āÄ v ‚Č™ volume`. All these volume/őĹ a.e. equalities transfer to `=ŠĶź[gaussianReal m‚āÄ v]` because `gaussianReal m‚āÄ v ‚Č™ volume` (`Measure.AbsolutelyContinuous.ae_eq` / `Filter.EventuallyEq.filter_mono` with `ae_mono`). For `.toReal`: `gaussianPDF m v x = ENNReal.ofReal (gaussianPDFReal m v x)` by def (`gaussianPDF`), `gaussianPDFReal_pos m‚āĀ v x hv` gives positivity so `ENNReal.toReal_div`/`ENNReal.toReal_ofReal` apply (both pdfs > 0, finite). It may be cleaner to combine steps (2)+(1): if computing `.toReal` is fiddly, you can instead prove `llr_gaussianReal_ae` directly from the ‚ĄĚ‚Č•0‚ąě rnDeriv. Use `lean_multi_attempt`/`lean_state_search` to find the exact `toReal_div`/`toReal_inv`/`toReal_mul` names.

(2) `llr_gaussianReal_ae` (L81): `llr őľ őĹ x = log ((őľ.rnDeriv őĹ x).toReal)` (`llr_def`). Using (1), `llr =ŠĶź x ‚Ü¶ log (gaussianPDFReal m‚āÄ v x / gaussianPDFReal m‚āĀ v x)`. Now `gaussianPDFReal m v x = (‚ąö(2*ŌÄ*v))‚ĀĽ¬Ļ * rexp(-(x-m)^2/(2*v))`. The `(‚ąö(2ŌÄv))‚ĀĽ¬Ļ` factors are identical (equal variance) and cancel in the quotient; `Real.log_div` (args ‚Č† 0, use `(gaussianPDFReal_pos ..).ne'`), `Real.log_mul`, `Real.log_exp`. After simplification the log equals `(-(x-m‚āÄ)^2 + (x-m‚āĀ)^2)/(2*v)`. Conclude `= (m‚āÄ-m‚āĀ)*(2*x-m‚āÄ-m‚āĀ)/(2*v)` by `ring` (note `(x-m‚āĀ)^2 - (x-m‚āÄ)^2 = (m‚āÄ-m‚āĀ)*(2*x-m‚āÄ-m‚āĀ)`). Note `(v:‚ĄĚ) > 0` so `2*v ‚Č† 0`.

(3) `integrable_llr_gaussianReal` (L90): use `(integrable_congr (llr_gaussianReal_ae m‚āÄ m‚āĀ hv)).mpr ?_` to reduce to integrability of the affine `x ‚Ü¶ (m‚āÄ-m‚āĀ)*(2*x-m‚āÄ-m‚āĀ)/(2*v)` under `gaussianReal m‚āÄ v`. That is a constant times `(2*x - (m‚āÄ+m‚āĀ))`; `Integrable id (gaussianReal m‚āÄ v)` comes from `(memLp_id_gaussianReal (őľ:=m‚āÄ) (v:=v) 1).integrable (by norm_num)` (or `le_rfl`); combine with `Integrable.const_mul`, `.add`/`.sub`, `integrable_const`. `id = fun x ‚Ü¶ x`. Massage with `simp only [...]`/`ring_nf` or rewrite the function into `fun x ‚Ü¶ c * x + d` shape first.

(4) `integral_llr_gaussianReal` (L97): `rw [integral_congr_ae (llr_gaussianReal_ae m‚āÄ m‚āĀ hv)]`, then integrate the affine function. Split the constant `1/(2*v)*(m‚āÄ-m‚āĀ)` out (`integral_const_mul`/`integral_mul_left`/`MeasureTheory.integral_div`), `integral_sub`/`integral_add` (needs integrability of pieces ‚ÄĒ `Integrable id` as in (3), `integrable_const`), `integral_id_gaussianReal : ‚ąę x ‚ąāgaussianReal m‚āÄ v = m‚āÄ`, and `‚ąę (c:‚ĄĚ) ‚ąāgaussianReal m‚āÄ v = c` since it's a probability measure (`integral_const`, `measure_univ`, `ENNReal.toReal_one`, `smul_eq_mul`, `mul_one`). Result `(m‚āÄ-m‚āĀ)*(2*m‚āÄ-m‚āÄ-m‚āĀ)/(2*v) = (m‚āÄ-m‚āĀ)^2/(2*v)` by `ring`/`field_simp`.

(5) `gaussianKL_eq` (L109): set `hv0 : v ‚Č† 0 := hv.ne'`. `rw [InformationTheory.klDiv_of_ac_of_integrable (gaussianReal_ac_gaussianReal m‚āÄ m‚āĀ hv0) (integrable_llr_gaussianReal m‚āÄ m‚āĀ hv0)]`. Then the argument of `ENNReal.ofReal` is `‚ąę llr ‚ąā(gaussianReal m‚āÄ v) + (gaussianReal m‚āĀ v).real Set.univ - (gaussianReal m‚āÄ v).real Set.univ`. Both `.real Set.univ = 1` via `probReal_univ` (these ARE probability measures; `measureReal_univ_eq_one` is the deprecated alias ‚ÄĒ prefer `probReal_univ`). So it becomes `‚ąę llr + 1 - 1 = ‚ąę llr`. `rw [integral_llr_gaussianReal m‚āÄ m‚āĀ hv0]` (or `congr 1; ...`) to reach `ENNReal.ofReal ((m‚āÄ-m‚āĀ)^2/(2*v))`. Use `simp`/`ring_nf` to discharge `+1-1`.

Use `lean_hover_info` to confirm exact signatures (argument order of `rnDeriv_withDensity_right`, `klDiv_of_ac_of_integrable`, `probReal_univ`), `lean_local_search`/`lean_loogle` to find any lemma whose exact name you are unsure of, and `lean_multi_attempt` to test tactic blocks before committing. Do NOT add new axioms, do NOT weaken statements, do NOT introduce `sorry` anywhere. Final success criterion: `lean_diagnostic_messages` on the file returns no errors and no `sorry` warnings."}]}

(2) `llr_gaussianReal_ae` (L81): `llr őľ őĹ x = log ((őľ.rnDeriv őĹ x).toReal)` (`llr_def`). Using (1), `llr =ŠĶź x ‚Ü¶ log (gaussianPDFReal m‚āÄ v x / gaussianPDFReal m‚āĀ v x)`. Now `gaussianPDFReal m v x = (‚ąö(2*ŌÄ*v))‚ĀĽ¬Ļ * rexp(-(x-m)^2/(2*v))`. The `(‚ąö(2ŌÄv))‚ĀĽ¬Ļ` factors are identical (equal variance) and cancel in the quotient; `Real.log_div` (args ‚Č† 0, use `(gaussianPDFReal_pos ..).ne'`), `Real.log_mul`, `Real.log_exp`. After simplification the log equals `(-(x-m‚āÄ)^2 + (x-m‚āĀ)^2)/(2*v)`. Conclude `= (m‚āÄ-m‚āĀ)*(2*x-m‚āÄ-m‚āĀ)/(2*v)` by `ring` (note `(x-m‚āĀ)^2 - (x-m‚āÄ)^2 = (m‚āÄ-m‚āĀ)*(2*x-m‚āÄ-m‚āĀ)`). Note `(v:‚ĄĚ) > 0` so `2*v ‚Č† 0`.

(3) `integrable_llr_gaussianReal` (L90): use `(integrable_congr (llr_gaussianReal_ae m‚āÄ m‚āĀ hv)).mpr ?_` to reduce to integrability of the affine `x ‚Ü¶ (m‚āÄ-m‚āĀ)*(2*x-m‚āÄ-m‚āĀ)/(2*v)` under `gaussianReal m‚āÄ v`. That is a constant times `(2*x - (m‚āÄ+m‚āĀ))`; `Integrable id (gaussianReal m‚āÄ v)` comes from `(memLp_id_gaussianReal (őľ:=m‚āÄ) (v:=v) 1).integrable (by norm_num)` (or `le_rfl`); combine with `Integrable.const_mul`, `.add`/`.sub`, `integrable_const`. `id = fun x ‚Ü¶ x`. (4) `integral_llr_gaussianReal` (L97): `rw [integral_congr_ae (llr_gaussianReal_ae m‚āÄ m‚āĀ hv)]`, then integrate the affine function. Split the constant `1/(2*v)*(m‚āÄ-m‚āĀ)` out (`integral_const_mul`/`integral_mul_left`/`MeasureTheory.integral_div`), `integral_sub`/`integral_add` (needs integrability of pieces ‚ÄĒ `Integrable id` as in (3), `integrable_const`), `integral_id_gaussianReal : ‚ąę x ‚ąāgaussianReal m‚āÄ v = m‚āÄ`, and `‚ąę (c:‚ĄĚ) ‚ąāgaussianReal m‚āÄ v = c` since it's a probability measure (`integral_const`, `measure_univ`, `ENNReal.toReal_one`, `smul_eq_mul`, `mul_one`). Result `(m‚āÄ-m‚āĀ)*(2*m‚āÄ-m‚āÄ-m‚āĀ)/(2*v) = (m‚āÄ-m‚āĀ)^2/(2*v)` by `ring`/`field_simp`.

Then the argument of `ENNReal.ofReal` is `‚ąę llr ‚ąā(gaussianReal m‚āÄ v) + (gaussianReal m‚āĀ v).real Set.univ - (gaussianReal m‚āÄ v).real Set.univ`. Both `.real Set.univ = 1` via `probReal_univ` (these ARE probability measures; `measureReal_univ_eq_one` is the deprecated alias ‚ÄĒ prefer `probReal_univ`).
